title: potentilla glandulosa subsp. reflexa
intro: Potentilla glandulosa subsp. reflexa, also known as glandular cinquefoil, is a perennial plant species within the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to western North America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, and is believed to have various health benefits such as soothing skin irritations and supporting the immune system. The leaves and roots of glandular cinquefoil are typically used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties. Additionally, the plant's compounds have been studied for their potential antioxidant and antiviral effects, although more research is needed to fully understand its benefits and applications.
